warps.notes: Left door tile on north side — exits to National Park warp 3
Right door tile on north side — exits to National Park warp 4
Left door tile on south side — exits to Route 35 warp 3 (x=3,y=5)
Right door tile on south side — also exits to Route 35 warp 3 (x=3,y=5)

signs.text: The Bug-Catching Contest is held on Tuesday, Thursday and Saturday. Not only do you earn a prize just for participating, you also get to keep the bug Pokemon you may have at the end of the contest.

tile_layout.notes: 20 columns x 8 rows. Gate building connecting Route 35 (south exits x=3-4,y=7) to National Park (north exits x=3-4,y=0). Contest Officer NPC at x=2,y=1 (visible on contest days: Tue/Thu/Sat). Youngster NPC at x=6,y=5 wanders (visible on non-contest days). Non-contest Officer at x=0,y=3 (visible on non-contest days). Sign at x=5,y=0 describes contest schedule. Contest mechanics fully scripted in Route35OfficerScriptContest.

npcs.name: Officer (Contest Day)
npcs.dialogue_summary: On contest days (Tue/Thu/Sat): Reads day of week from VAR_WEEKDAY, asks if player wants to participate. Handles full contest entry flow: party management (only first Pokemon allowed), gives Park Balls, explains rules (20 min time limit), warps player into NATIONAL_PARK_BUG_CONTEST. If contest already done today (ENGINE_DAILY_BUG_CONTEST): 'Today's Contest is over.' If player leaving early (ENGINE_BUG_CONTEST_TIMER active): asks if they want to finish, then warps to results or sends back.
special_functions: ContestDropOffMons (deposits excess party to contest helper)
npcs.notes: Only appears (via MAPCALLBACK_OBJECTS) when the day of week is Tuesday, Thursday, or Saturday. Uses VAR_WEEKDAY. Contest runs for BUG_CONTEST_MINUTES. Player uses BUG_CONTEST_BALLS Park Balls.

npcs.name: Youngster
npcs.dialogue_summary: When is the next Bug-Catching Contest going to be? (Appears on non-contest days only.)

npcs.name: Officer (Non-Contest Day)
npcs.dialogue_summary: We hold Contests regularly in the PARK. You should give it a shot. (Appears on non-contest days: Sun/Mon/Wed/Fri.)

map_callbacks.notes: On entering the map: checks ENGINE_BUG_CONTEST_TIMER flag. If contest is actively running, sets SCENE_ROUTE35NATIONALPARKGATE_LEAVE_CONTEST_EARLY (triggers leave-early dialog scene). Otherwise sets SCENE_ROUTE35NATIONALPARKGATE_NOOP.
Checks VAR_WEEKDAY. If Tue/Thu/Sat: shows Officer1 (contest), hides Youngster and Officer2. Otherwise (and not mid-contest): hides Officer1, shows Youngster and Officer2.

scene_scripts.description: No-op scene (end). Default state when not mid-contest.

Triggered when player exits park mid-contest (ENGINE_BUG_CONTEST_TIMER active). Officer1 intercepts, asks if they want to finish. If yes: warps to contest results. If no: sends player back into the park.

story_events.description: Player arrives at National Park Gate from Route 35. On a non-contest day, Officer2 tells them about contests. On a contest day, Officer1 offers contest entry.
story_events.notes: Gate is freely traversable in both directions outside contest hours.

story_events.description: On contest days, Officer1 manages entry: reads day, confirms participation, handles party setup (only first Pokemon used — others held by Contest Helper), gives Park Balls (BUG_CONTEST_BALLS), explains 20-minute rules, warps player into National Park Bug Contest area.
If player re-enters the gate while ENGINE_BUG_CONTEST_TIMER is active (contest still running), Officer1 intercepts. Player can choose to forfeit (warp to results) or return to the park.
Once ENGINE_DAILY_BUG_CONTEST is set for the day, Officer1 tells player the contest is over and to come back another time.

notes: The Route 35 / National Park Gate is a 10x4 block (20x8 tile) gate building forming the sole southern entrance to the National Park from Route 35. It is the hub for the Bug-Catching Contest system — the most complex scripted recurring event in Johto. Contest runs Tuesdays, Thursdays, and Saturdays. The gate has three NPC variants managed by MAPCALLBACK_OBJECTS: Officer1 (contest days), Officer2 + Youngster (non-contest days). The MAPCALLBACK_NEWMAP callback detects if the player is exiting mid-contest (ENGINE_BUG_CONTEST_TIMER) and triggers a scene that forces them to decide whether to forfeit. Party management is fully automated: on entry with more than one Pokemon, extras are deposited with the Contest Helper. Contest warps the player to NATIONAL_PARK_BUG_CONTEST at coordinates (10,47) via warpfacing UP. A sign on the north wall explains contest schedule and prizes.
